cache_key_change.rationale: distinct tokens get distinct cache entries (no cross-tenant cache poisoning); raw token never appears in any filename

verbose_redaction_pattern.mechanism: bash parameter substitution `${msg//${BRIDGE_HELPER_AUTH}/<REDACTED>}` strips literal occurrences of the auth value from every verbose log line

test_1_auth_on_wire.evidence: mock server (127.0.0.1:8765) saw `Authorization: Bearer test123` in request headers

test_2_no_leak_in_verbose.evidence: with BRIDGE_HELPER_VERBOSE=1, full stderr trace contained zero occurrences of literal token 'test123'; log line read 'AUTH header configured via tempfile (value redacted)'

test_3_cache_key_respects_auth.evidence: same URL fetched with `Bearer test123` then `Bearer DIFFERENT_TOKEN` produced two distinct .body files (mock1_<urlhash>_acf7af531.body and mock1_<urlhash>_ac067a771.body); same URL+same auth refetch produced CACHE HIT

test_4_backward_compat.evidence: with BRIDGE_HELPER_AUTH unset, cache filename was `mockX_47d9e62e460b2b0c.body` (no _a suffix); behaviour identical to ω-bridge-6 baseline

extra_argv_safety_check.evidence: bash -x trace of curl invocation showed `--header @/var/.../tmp.5nf2MgXLiy` — only the tempfile path is in argv, the secret value is not. ps-safe.

extra_tempfile_cleanup_check.evidence: post-process inspection of tempfile path showed file was removed by the EXIT trap

security_notes.tempfile_cleanup: trap "rm -f '$_auth_tmp' 2>/dev/null" EXIT — fires on success, deterministic 4xx, persistent fail, and any uncaught error
security_notes.cache_filename_exposure: none — only sha1(auth)[0:8] suffix; raw token never on disk except in the ephemeral mode-0600 tempfile
security_notes.cache_body_exposure: the response body is cached as-is — if the upstream API echoes the token in its response (rare; http://httpbin.org/headers does this), the token would land on disk in the cached body. Caller responsibility to flush cache for any bridge whose API echoes secrets back. Documented future-work item.
security_notes.verbose_redaction_scope: literal-string substring replacement; covers the common case of curl/bridge logs echoing the header. Does NOT redact partial substrings of the token (full match only).

rollback.alternative: unset BRIDGE_HELPER_AUTH — when env var is unset the new code paths are no-ops and behaviour is byte-identical to ω-bridge-6 (sha256 5b529b42...)
rollback.blast_radius: zero existing bridge calls (none currently set BRIDGE_HELPER_AUTH); cache key suffix is conditional on auth presence; backward-compat verified in spot test 4

constraints_honored.no_live_api_hammering: true — used local python http.server mock on 127.0.0.1:8765
